name: ESP32 / ESP-IDF
description: Espressif ESP32 series (ESP32/S3/S2/C3/C6/H2), WiFi+BLE dual-core Xtensa/RISC-V
common_pitfalls: Core 0 WiFi/LwIP protocol stack is non-preemptible, user tasks recommended to bind to Core 1
PSRAM DMA limitation: SPI DMA does not support PSRAM, I2S DMA buffers must be in Internal SRAM
Task WDT monitors esp_timer + main (app_main) by default, prolonged blocking triggers WDT reset
esp_websocket_client callback runs in esp_event task context (not UI thread), lv_obj_* operations are forbidden
ESP32-S3 has USB Serial/JTAG for OTA, no additional USB chip needed
CONFIG_FREERTOS_HZ defaults to 1000 (tick=1ms), affecting vTaskDelay precision
xTaskCreate stack units are words (4 bytes/word), not bytes
MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM and MALLOC_CAP_DMA in heap_caps_malloc cannot be mixed
Camera/LCD/I2S DMA buffers must verify internal SRAM, MALLOC_CAP_DMA, and cache clean/invalidate direction
After idf.py build, use idf.py size-components to check Flash/RAM usage of each component
When BLE + WiFi coexist, BLE priority is higher than WiFi, which may affect WSS real-time performance

wss_tls.note: mbedTLS handshake peak memory is high, ESP-IDF 5.x recommends >=6144

av_sync.note: audio clock master + camera/video frame queue

media_dma_buffer.note: DMA-capable frame pool + cache sync bookkeeping

pinmux_notes.gpio_0: BOOT strapping pin, must be high at power-on
pinmux_notes.gpio_6_11: SPI0/SPI1 Flash, cannot be used as regular GPIO
pinmux_notes.gpio_12: Strapping pin (Flash voltage select), must be low at boot
pinmux_notes.gpio_34_39: Input only (ADC2/WiFi), cannot be configured as output
